2.q: MpPUB9 was also found to directly interact with the exocyst protein MpEXO70.1, leading to its ubiquitination.

3.q: T-DNA insertional knockdown mutants for MpFER and MpMRI, the unique homologs of the MLR and RLCK-VIII families in Marchantia, respectively, were found in a large genetic screen for Marchantia mutants with short rhizoids.

4.q: MpFER and MpMRI, the unique homologs of the MLR and RLCK-VIII families in Marchantia... control root hair and rhizoid integrity... the MLR/MRI signaling module that controls CWI in tip-growing cells.

6.q: MpEXO70.1 was identified as a target substrate of MpPUB9, which mediated the 26S proteasome‐dependent degradation of MpEXO70.1.

7.q: All M. polymorpha CBL-CIPKs interact with each other in planta [BiFC and yeast two-hybrid].

8.q: All M. polymorpha CBL-CIPKs interact with each other in planta [BiFC and yeast two-hybrid].

9.q: All M. polymorpha CBL-CIPKs interact with each other in planta [BiFC and yeast two-hybrid].

10.q: All M. polymorpha CBL-CIPKs interact with each other in planta [BiFC and yeast two-hybrid].

11.q: All M. polymorpha CBL-CIPKs interact with each other in planta [BiFC and yeast two-hybrid].

12.q: We identified MpDCL1a in microRNA biogenesis, MpDCL4 in trans-acting small interfering RNA generation, and MpDCL3 in the regulation of pathogen-related genes.

13.q: We confirm that M. polymorpha has two CIPKs and three CBLs. Both CIPKs and one CBL show pronounced salt-responsive transcriptional changes.
